response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented full-stack engineer with recent big-tech experience and genuine low-latency systems exposure, but he misses the role's core minimum qualification by a wide margin: professional C++ amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, against a required 8 years. His infrastructure work is real (micro-services handling millions of requests per second, Kubernetes deployments, AWS CDK) yet skews toward application and product engineering rather than owning cloud compute platform architecture, and his leadership evidence is workshops and mentoring rather than a formal technical-lead role driving cross-team strategy.
strengths: Hands-on low-latency distributed systems experience (async I/O, caching, micro-services at millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored open-source testing frameworks
Proven mentor and knowledge-sharer with workshops, courses, and conference talks, plus recent Staff-level big-tech experience
concerns: Far short of the 8-year C++ minimum; primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
Little evidence of designing or operating a large-scale cloud compute platform; cloud experience is product-adjacent (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deploys)
No formal technical leadership role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment for infrastructure teams

key_factors.factor: Informal rather than formal technical leadership
